title: Architecture of LLM-Assisted ESG Disclosure Assessment Framework
caption: Schematic overview illustrating the technical workflow of the proposed LLM-assisted ESG assessment system, including multilingual ESG document input, Taiwan-specific fine-tuning of the multilingual baseline model, expert human evaluation integration, and quantitative evaluation pipeline.
data_source: Constructed schematic based on methodology section detailing model and evaluation pipeline
notes: Diagram should visually represent the input data flow (Mandarin, English), fine-tuning steps, model inference, comparison with human expert assessments, and output metrics. Use clear icons for LLM, datasets, evaluation, and bilingual inputs.

title: Comparison of LLM-Assisted and Human Expert ESG Assessment Performance
caption: Bar charts comparing accuracy, inter-rater reliability (Fleiss’ Kappa), and average processing time for ESG disclosure evaluations by LLM-assisted methods versus traditional human-only approaches on Taiwan-listed companies.
data_source: Empirical evaluation results from experiments comparing LLM-assisted and human assessments

axes.y: Metric Value (Accuracy %, Fleiss’ Kappa, Processing Time in Hours)

notes: Include three grouped bar charts side-by-side or stacked for the three metrics. Use consistent color coding for human and LLM. Add error bars if statistical significance data available.

title: Error Type Analysis in ESG Assessment: Discrepancies between LLM and Human Evaluations
caption: Breakdown of common error categories observed in ESG assessment discrepancies, highlighting challenges arising from bilingual disclosures and domain-specific language, with example illustrative cases.
data_source: Evaluation data highlighting discrepancies between human and LLM assessments focusing on error typology

axes.x: Error Type (e.g., Misclassification, Language Ambiguity, Regulatory Term Confusion, Missing Context)

notes: Use a bar chart to depict frequency counts of different error categories. Include textual callouts or small insets showing sample bilingual text excerpts causing errors.
